name: sqlsmith
title: sqlsmith
description:
random SQL query generator
SQLsmith is a random SQL query generator. Its paragon is Csmith, which proved
valuable for quality assurance in C compilers.
.
It currently supports generating queries for PostgreSQL 9.5+ and sqlite3.
.
Besides PostgreSQL developers, users developing extensions of PostgreSQL might
also be interested in exposing their code to SQLsmith's random workload.
.
During its prototyping stage, it already found about thirty bugs in
PostgreSQL alphas, betas and releases, including security vulnerabilities in
released versions.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sqlsmith because random SQL query generator.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sqlsmith as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sqlsmith [options] [args]` as documented upstream for sqlsmith.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sqlsmith with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sqlsmith --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
